question: A select element is bound via **Key** `priority` to an existing process variable whose value is `"urgent"`, but the configured options source only defines `low`, `medium` and `high`. What does the user see when the form renders?

options.text: The form fails to render and an error is shown to the user.
A new option `urgent` is added to the dropdown at render time.
The bound value `urgent` is ignored and no option is pre-selected.
The first defined option (`low`) is pre-selected as a fallback.

a.text: Form rendering does not fail when the bound value has no matching option; the docs describe an explicit ignore-on-mismatch behaviour.

b.text: The select does not synthesise new options from the bound data; the option set comes from the configured Options source.

c.text: Per the Datatypes section, the select correlates the bound data value with the appropriate option from the options source. If no matching option is found, the data is simply ignored.

d.text: There is no documented fallback to the first option; the field renders with no pre-selection when the value does not match.

explanation: The select correlates the bound data value with its options. When the value does not match any option in the configured Options source — here `urgent` against `low`/`medium`/`high` — the docs say the data is simply ignored, so the dropdown renders with no pre-selection.
